title: Woodsia hyperborea
intro: Woodsia hyperborea, also known as the alpine woodsia or tundra woodsia, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Woodsiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Woodsia hyperborea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Woodsia hyperborea is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, and it is also used in some traditional remedies, such as infusions and tinctures, to leverage its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Woodsia hyperborea is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae. It belongs to the genus Woodsia, which comprises a group of small to medium-sized ferns. Woodsia hyperborea is native to the Arctic tundra regions of North America and Eurasia. The classification of Woodsia hyperborea is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This classification reflects its unique characteristics as a vascular plant that reproduces via spores.
